---
format: html
title: extract_anova_fractions()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>extract_anova_fractions</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Extract ANOVA Fraction Components from a Single Object</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Extracts specified variance partitioning fractions from a
single sjSDManova object, returning a two-column tibble with
fraction codes and their Nagelkerke R² values.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>extract_anova_fractions(
  anova_object,
  vec_anova_fractions = c("F_A", "F_B", "F_S", "F_AB", "F_AS", "F_BS", "F_ABS"),
  clamp_negative = TRUE
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="anova_object">anova_object</code></td>
<td>
<p>A single sjSDManova-like object containing a
<code>$results</code> element with columns <code>models</code> and
<code>"R2 Nagelkerke"</code>. Must be a list.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="vec_anova_fractions">vec_anova_fractions</code></td>
<td>
<p>A non-empty character vector of fraction codes to retain
(e.g. <code>c("F_A", "F_B", "F_S")</code>).</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="clamp_negative">clamp_negative</code></td>
<td>
<p>A single logical (default <code>TRUE</code>). If <code>TRUE</code>,
negative Nagelkerke R² values are clamped to 0.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>Accesses <code>anova_object$results</code> via
<code>purrr::chuck()</code>, filters rows whose <code>models</code>
value is in <code>vec_anova_fractions</code>, renames the
columns to <code>component</code> and <code>R2_Nagelkerke</code>,
translates internal fraction codes to human-readable
labels, and (when <code>clamp_negative = TRUE</code>) clamps
negative R² values to 0:
</p>

<ul>
<li><p> F_A   -&gt; &quot;Abiotic&quot;
</p>
</li>
<li><p> F_B   -&gt; &quot;Associations&quot;
</p>
</li>
<li><p> F_S   -&gt; &quot;Spatial&quot;
</p>
</li>
<li><p> F_AB  -&gt; &quot;Abiotic&amp;Associations&quot;
</p>
</li>
<li><p> F_AS  -&gt; &quot;Abiotic&amp;Spatial&quot;
</p>
</li>
<li><p> F_BS  -&gt; &quot;Associations&amp;Spatial&quot;
</p>
</li>
<li><p> F_ABS -&gt; &quot;Abiotic&amp;Associations&amp;Spatial&quot;
</p>
</li></ul>



<h3>Value</h3>

<p>A tibble with columns:
</p>

<dl>
<dt>component</dt><dd><p>Character. Human-readable component label
(e.g. &quot;Abiotic&quot;, &quot;Associations&quot;, &quot;Spatial&quot;).</p>
</dd>
<dt>R2_Nagelkerke</dt><dd><p>Numeric. Nagelkerke R² value for
each fraction, optionally clamped to [0, Inf).</p>
</dd>
</dl>



<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>[aggregate_anova_components()]
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
